note: SNG-090 — Erik-directed 2026-07-12. EXTENDS existing canon (`power_systems.md`: *'All power in this world is nanite-mediated'*; `will_modality.json`: *'denser near the deep sites, thinner in the safe margins'*). What was MISSING: **peoples differ in how much substrate they NEED.**
thePhysics: Every craft in this world is nanite-mediated. There is no magic — there is a Precursor lattice that reads structured will and enacts it. **The crafts ARE technology, and the traditions are the disciplines that learned to speak to it.**
theCorrection: ⛔ The Seraphic Orders are NOT the only people who never abandoned the power. Several did not. **But those who DID abandon it decreased the potential of those powers** — and in exchange, they can run on almost nothing. **This is the central tradeoff of the world and it was never mechanical until now.**

theTwoNumbers.substrateBand: A TRADITION's TUNING — a center and a width. **Inside the band: full power. Below: STARVATION (steep). Above: INTERFERENCE (mild).**
theTwoNumbers.substrateDensity: A PLACE's supply. 0.0 = true nature, the lattice is gone. 1.0 = the deep sites.
theTwoNumbers.theRule: **effective = f(distance from the band, and WHICH SIDE).** Below-band falloff is steep and can reach near-zero. Above-band falloff is gentle and floors around 0.6–0.75. **A craft is never switched off by ABUNDANCE — only by absence.**
theTwoNumbers.carried: Carried charge RAISES effective local density. **It is not universally good: it helps whoever is below their band and HARMS whoever is above it.**

theTradeoff.theContinuous: Never abandoned it. **Highest ceiling in the world — and helpless where the lattice is gone.** Enginewrights, the Lattice-Cities, the Figurists, the SERAPHIC ORDERS, the Blazeborn, the Cogitants, the Hourkeepers, the Numinous, the Veilwrights.
theTradeoff.theReturned: Went back to true nature. **Lower ceiling — and they work ANYWHERE.** The Rootkin, the Somatics, the Marchers, the Valleyfolk, the Churnfolk, the Masons, the Ashwardens, the Stillhold.
theTradeoff.theKeyImage: ⛔ **A SERAPH IN THE QUICKWOOD IS NEARLY POWERLESS.** Luminous, certain, terrifying — and reduced to a person with excellent posture and a very strong opinion. Meanwhile a Rootkin in the Gearlands is *fine*, because their power was never borrowed from the lattice in the first place. **The Seraph has only what ambient, dormant substrate lies nearby — and what they BROUGHT WITH THEM.**

carriedSubstrate.principle: ⛔ **YOU CAN CARRY YOUR OWN SUPPLY.** This is the logistics layer of the whole world, and it has been sitting in Erik's inventory the entire time.
carriedSubstrate.theWaystaff: *'resonance-crystal translator staff' · 'three crystals charged, one shallow' · 'three charged nodes'* — **the Waystaff is a NANITE BATTERY.** Erik has been carrying his own substrate since the first session and neither of us said it out loud.
carriedSubstrate.aevi: The companion Aevi is *'a curious swarm of nanite-motes'* — **a living, mobile substrate source.** A Continuous-tradition character travelling with Aevi is carrying their power supply, and it has opinions.
implications: Charged reservoirs are the most valuable trade goods in the world, and the Crossing's Hundred Markets run on them.
An expedition into the Quickwood by a Continuous people is a LOGISTICS problem before it is a combat one.
Running out of charge in a low-density region is a survival scenario, not an inconvenience.
**The Rootkin have never needed to buy a charge in their lives, and they find the whole trade faintly ridiculous.**

whyTheQuickwoodIsEMPTY: The Rootkin did not merely go back to nature — **they grew a country the lattice cannot get purchase in.** Living wood, root-fed water, four hundred years of deliberate biological growth. It is the single lowest-substrate region in the world (0.12) and **that is not an accident. It is a fortification, and the Lattice-Cities know it.**
strategicConsequences: **The map is now TWO difficulty maps.** Dispositional misfit (SNG-079: how far a place is from what you ARE) AND substrate density (whether your craft can run at all). A place can be easy for you dispositionally and still switch you off.
**It explains the Blaze.** A high-density region, expanding — and it is *sterilising the substrate into glass* as it goes. The Blazeborn are burning their own supply.

**It explains the Deep Works.** Masons [concrete] and Enginewrights [mechanical] sit on the densest ground in the world. **The Masons are LOW-dependency (0.32) mining the HIGHEST-density substrate — they hold the supply and do not need it.** That is the most powerful strategic position on the map and nobody has named it.
**It gives the Accords teeth.** A waygate is not a convenience; **it is a supply line.**
**It is why the Numinous built the waygates** — and why they do not use them. They are dissolving. They no longer need to arrive anywhere.

AMENDMENT_1.raisedBy: Erik, via CCode's ROUND 2 (2026-07-12). **Aevi's original model was a ONE-SIDED FUEL GAUGE — more substrate was always better, up to a cap. That was wrong.**
AMENDMENT_1.theCorrection: ⛔ **IT IS A BAND, NOT A GAUGE.** Every tradition has a substrate level it is TUNED TO, and it falls off in **BOTH** directions. **You can have too much.**
AMENDMENT_1.why: The Returned did not merely learn to survive on less — **they RETUNED.** A Rootkin is not a starving Enginewright; they are a people whose craft is calibrated to thin ground. Drop them in the Gearlands and the lattice does not empower them — **it INTERFERES.** Too much signal is noise.
AMENDMENT_1.asymmetry: **Equivalent, not equal.** Starvation is STEEP (a Continuous craft below its band goes toward OFF — a Seraph in the Quickwood ≈13%). Interference is MILDER (a Returned craft above its band is impaired but never switched off — floor ~60–75%).
AMENDMENT_1.theEmergentBonus: ⛔ **CARRIED SUBSTRATE CAN HURT YOU.** A battery RAISES local density — which pulls a Continuous craft UP into its band (good) and can push a Returned craft ABOVE its band (bad). **THIS IS WHY THE ROOTKIN FIND THE CHARGE TRADE RIDICULOUS.** They are not being provincial. **They are being correct: the thing everyone is buying would make them worse.**
AMENDMENT_1.creditWhereDue: CCode surfaced this in ROUND 2 rather than building Aevi's flawed model. **That is exactly what ROUND 2 is for, and it is the third time this cycle it has caught the PO.**

tuningNote: ⚠️ THE CURVES ARE NOT TUNED. Band widths are a first pass (narrow for the Continuous, wide for the Returned). **SNG-078's balance harness must exist before these numbers are trusted — do NOT eyeball them.** (CCode's ROUND-2 blocker, accepted.)
separateTerms: ⛔ **SUBSTRATE AND SPECTRAL FIT (SNG-079) ARE SEPARATE TERMS AND MUST NEVER BE FOLDED TOGETHER.** The resolve chain already carries a place-disposition term. Substrate is a SECOND, independent environmental term. *A place can suit you dispositionally and still starve your craft.* (CCode's ROUND-2 finding, accepted.)

threeGrounds.note: Erik's ruling 2026-07-19 — closing the substrate geography. Low density has THREE causes and they are not interchangeable. A place reads thin for a reason, and the reason is a fact about its history.
threeGrounds.pooled: UNTRANSITIONED — the Transition never took here, so the lattice collected and stayed. Dense. The Gearlands, the Deep Works, the Great Engine. Authored as substrateSource kind:pool.
threeGrounds.released: COMPLETED — the Returned finished the Transition and the lattice WITHDREW, deliberately. Thin, and thin as an achievement. The Quickwood, the Flesh Temple, the Stillhold. Authored as substrateSource kind:sink.
threeGrounds.unreached: NEVER ARRIVED — the nanites and the infrastructure simply never made it out this far. Thin because nothing was ever built, not because anything was gained or lost. This is the DEFAULT state of ordinary country and needs no authored source; it is what the regional ambient already describes.
threeGrounds.whyItMatters: Released ground and unreached ground read the same on a gauge and are opposite in meaning. One is a people's completed work; the other is the edge of the map. A GM should never describe them the same way.
threeGrounds.wildHabitat: The unreached gaps are where WILD nanites thrive — ungoverned, untended, and answerable to no Accord. Sparse authored coverage (43 of 95 locations carry local variation) is not a gap in the model: it is room for wild current, and room for locations not yet authored.
threeGrounds.coverage: 43 of 95 locations carry authored local variation as of 2026-07-19. The remaining 52 read their regional ambient, which is correct — most ground is ordinary and the anomalies are the point.

bandAudit.note: SNG-172 / SNG-179 follow-up, 2026-07-19. power_sources.json does NOT drive this table — it AUDITS it. The two are independently authored and their agreement is the check: a natural-source tradition should carry a LOW band centre (thin ground is its good ground) and a lattice-source tradition a HIGH one. Where they disagree, one of the two is wrong and a human should look.
bandAudit.agreements: rootkin 0.18, somatic 0.20, marcher 0.26, mason 0.32, ashwarden 0.35 — all natural, all low. Erik's ruling that natural craft benefits from thin lattice is ALREADY mechanically true for these five.
disagreements: umbral — had NO band at all, the only pole tradition missing one. Filled here at 0.58/0.28 to match its region (0.58) and the established mid-tier width. Until this fill it was NEUTRAL: full power at every density.

threnodist 0.50 and verist 0.50 — classified natural but banded mid. Possibly correct (feeling and truth-telling are not thin-ground crafts in the way growing and fighting are), possibly a gap. NOT changed; flagged for Erik.
bandAudit.whyNotWired: Making power_sources DRIVE the band would mean an inverted curve for natural sources — new curve shape, which is under tuningNote and Erik's balance call. It is not needed: band centre already expresses the same fact, and a CI check that the two agree is cheaper, needs no new curve, and catches exactly the kind of hole umbral was.
